⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔ ꒰ ᧔ෆ᧓ ꒱ ⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔

✦ 𝐁𝐥𝐮𝐫𝐛 ✦

The world is a place of power, and power has its own set of rules—most people just never learn them.

Hunters are licensed problem solvers with frightening amounts of freedom. They can go wherever they want, dig into whatever they want, and get paid ridiculous amounts of money to do it. Some hunt criminals, some hunt monsters, some hunt knowledge itself. It’s a job that attracts geniuses, madmen, idealists, and people who should never, ever be trusted with power.

Gon Freecss enters this world with a smile on his face, his feet bare, and a complete lack of preparation, looking for his missing father and determined to prove to himself that the world is not cruel until it proves otherwise. Killua Zoldyck, a boy raised to kill before he learned how to play, sticks with him—bored, brilliant, and slowly realizing that having a friend isn’t supposed to hurt. Kurapika walks with purpose and quiet anger, carrying a promise that will get him killed young if he can keep it. Leorio is along for the money, but it’s clear he’s lying to himself about that.

And then there’s Nen. The explanation for why some people become gods and others die trying. It rewards obsession, punishes hesitation, and makes your worst traits dangerously effective. Talent matters. Willpower matters more. What you’re willing to sacrifice matters most.

This isn’t a heroic journey so much as a slow exposure to how ugly ambition can get—and how much it costs to stay kind anyway. People don’t “level up.” They break, adapt, or disappear. Sometimes they win. Sometimes they survive. Sometimes they learn exactly who they are and hate the answer.

⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔ ꒰ ᧔ෆ᧓ ꒱ ⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔⏔
